commands.author: TurboTheTurtle

target.title: Improve doctor runtime and schema drift diagnostics
target.body:
Closes #85.

Adds read-only doctor JSON diagnostics for the active executable/runtime identity and local DB schema state, including supported/current versions, pending/newer migration flags, PR-detail storage support, and concise drift next steps.

Keeps schema inspection diagnostic-only so `doctor --json` can report missing, newer, or legacy stores without applying migrations.

Doctor JSON contract: scripts should inspect `db_schema.state` and `source_db_schema.state` for schema health. When `runtime_open_error` is present, `doctor --json` still emits the runtime identity plus read-only schema diagnostics so automation can report drift instead of losing the diagnostic payload.

Behavior proof from a branch-built macOS arm64 binary at `0a02a2ae636e`:

Current database excerpt:
```json
{
  "runtime": {
    "build_revision": "0a02a2ae636e7f7da4b491acb2f53cc109e9bc77",
    "module_path": "github.com/openclaw/gitcrawl",
    "version": "dev"
  },
  "db_schema": {
    "current_version": 4,
    "supported_version": 4,
    "state": "current",
    "current": true,
    "pending_migration": false,
    "newer": false,
    "pr_details": {
      "files_position_key": true,
      "duplicate_path_files_supported": true,
      "state": "supported"
    }
  }
}
```

Legacy drift excerpt:
```json
{
  "db_schema": {
    "current_version": 3,
    "supported_version": 4,
    "state": "pending_migration",
    "pending_migration": true,
    "legacy": true,
    "pending_migrations": ["schema_version_3_to_4"],
    "next_steps": ["Run a write-path command with the intended rebuilt gitcrawl executable to apply pending migrations."]
  }
}
```

Newer drift excerpt:
```json
{
  "db_schema": {
    "current_version": 99,
    "supported_version": 4,
    "state": "newer",
    "newer": true,
    "next_steps": ["Upgrade the active gitcrawl executable to a build that supports this database schema."]
  },
  "runtime_open_error": "database schema version 99 is newer than supported version 4"
}
```
target.author: turbotheturtle
